compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Context / Problem

The AWS Gerrit `LLM-Review` bot (`src/rebar/review_bot/`) makes billable LLM calls on
every patchset review but records **no token usage** in any retrievable form. Per-call token
usage is computed by the runner (`_extract_usage` → `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`,
`cache_read_tokens`, `cache_write_tokens`, `requests`; `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`) and attached
as `result["_usage"]`, but the review path discards the token fields: the code-review metrics
assembler reads `_usage` only for the verifier **request count**
(`src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize.py:157`, `verify_requests`), and the voter never reads
usage at all. The bot already publishes CloudWatch *error* counts via a best-effort boto3
pattern (`src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py:88-102`, `:145-165`), so there is an established seam
to emit token counts the same way — it is simply not wired for usage.

Goal: have the bot read `_usage` and emit token counts to CloudWatch.

**Coverage scope (explicit).** This ticket captures the token usage that is already surfaced
on the review's **agent** step outputs — Pass-2 `verify` and Pass-3 `decide`
(`finalize.py:143-162` already iterates these and reads their `_usage`). The Pass-1 **finder
batch** does NOT surface per-finder `_usage` on its step output
(`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py:168-177` emits `findings`/`criteria_count`
but no `_usage`; the shared `run_pass1` returns only `findings`,
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py:548`), and threading finder usage through that shared
plan-review path is a larger, riskier change. So the emitted totals are the review's
agent-step token usage, labelled as such, and a follow-up ticket is filed (linked
`discovered_from`) to surface Pass-1 finder usage for a complete total. This is strictly
better than today's zero token visibility and does not misrepresent the number.

Edit-set:
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize.py`
- `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py`
- *new* `tests/unit/test_review_bot_token_metrics.py`

## Approach

1. `finalize.py` `_attach_code_review_metrics`: in the existing `rec.steps` loop that already
   reads `outputs["_usage"]`, aggregate the token fields across **every succeeded step that
   exposes `_usage`** (not just the verify request count). Add to `coverage["metrics"]`:
   `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read_tokens`, `cache_write_tokens`, and
   `total_tokens` (= input + output). Keep `verify_requests` unchanged. This also enriches the
   persisted `code_review` artifact (which serializes `coverage.metrics`), so the data is
   retrievable both in CloudWatch and in the artifact. The addition is additive and tolerant of
   steps without `_usage` (contributes 0).
2. `voter.py`: add `_publish_token_usage_metrics(metrics: dict)` — best-effort boto3
   `put_metric_data` to `Namespace="rebar/host"`, one `MetricData` entry per token field
   (`review_bot_llm_input_tokens`, `review_bot_llm_output_tokens`,
   `review_bot_llm_cache_read_tokens`, `review_bot_llm_cache_write_tokens`,
   `review_bot_llm_total_tokens`; `Unit: "Count"`), swallowing any ImportError/boto/credential
   error exactly like `_publish_voter_error_metric` (`voter.py:88-102`). Also emit a greppable
   `LLM_TOKEN_USAGE ` JSON line to stderr/journald for the reliable host-probe fallback,
   mirroring the error markers. Call it after a **successful** vote (in `review_and_vote`, right
   after the `voter_voted` emit, ~`voter.py:644-657`), reading the totals from
   `decision["verdict"]["coverage"]["metrics"]` (the verdict is threaded up at
   `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py:219`). Best-effort: the vote is already cast, so emission
   never fails the review (wrapped, swallowed, logged) — same contract as artifact emission.

Rejected: threading Pass-1 finder `_usage` through `run_pass1` now (shared plan-review path,
signature change, thread-pool aggregation) — deferred to the linked follow-up. Rejected a
process-global `REBAR_USAGE_LOG`/accumulator to capture all calls — the bot reviews changes
concurrently, so a process-global sink would interleave usage across reviews.

## Scope

- Emits **token counts only** (input/output/cache/total tokens + the existing request count).
  No dollar cost (no token→USD price table exists in the repo).
- CloudWatch emission is **best-effort** and swallowed on any failure — identical fail-open
  contract to the existing error-metric publishers; a metric failure never affects the vote.
- Covers Pass-2 verify + Pass-3 decide agent-step usage (see Coverage scope above); Pass-1
  finder usage is a linked follow-up.
- No change to voting logic, thresholds, dedup, or the artifact schema beyond the additive
  `coverage.metrics` token keys.

## Testing

New `tests/unit/test_review_bot_token_metrics.py` (offline; no live LLM, no real AWS):
- `finalize`: build a fake step-recorder (`rec.steps`) with agent steps carrying `_usage`
  dicts and assert `_attach_code_review_metrics` sums `input_tokens`/`output_tokens`/
  `cache_read_tokens`/`cache_write_tokens`/`total_tokens` into `coverage["metrics"]`, and that a
  record with no `_usage` yields zeros (tolerant). Mirrors the metrics assertions in
  `tests/unit/test_code_review_fp_ledger.py` / `tests/unit/test_findings_finalize.py`.
- `voter`: monkeypatch a fake `boto3` module capturing `put_metric_data` calls and assert
  `_publish_token_usage_metrics({...})` emits the five named metrics with the right values to
  `Namespace="rebar/host"`; assert that when `boto3` import/credentials fail the call swallows
  the error (returns without raising). Mirrors the boto3-absence tolerance the existing
  publishers document (`voter.py:101`).
- Optionally assert (via the existing `review_and_vote` test harness in
  `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py`) that a successful vote invokes the token publisher with the
  verdict's `coverage.metrics`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_attach_code_review_metrics` adds `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read_tokens`,
      `cache_write_tokens`, and `total_tokens` to `coverage["metrics"]`, summed across all
      `_usage`-bearing succeeded steps; verified by the finalize unit test asserting exact sums.
- [ ] A step record with no `_usage` contributes 0 and never raises; verified by the tolerance
      unit test.
- [ ] `voter._publish_token_usage_metrics` emits exactly the five `review_bot_llm_*_tokens`
      metrics to `Namespace="rebar/host"` with `Unit="Count"` and values from the passed
      metrics dict; verified by the fake-boto3 unit test capturing `put_metric_data`.
- [ ] `_publish_token_usage_metrics` swallows a boto3 import/credential/network failure without
      raising; verified by the boto3-absence unit test.
- [ ] `review_and_vote` calls the token publisher after a successful vote using
      `decision["verdict"]["coverage"]["metrics"]`; verified by the voter harness test (or an
      equivalent direct assertion).
- [ ] A follow-up ticket for Pass-1 finder token capture is created and linked
      `discovered_from` this ticket.
- [ ] `make lint`, `make typecheck`, and `make test` pass; `finalize.py` and `voter.py` stay
      ≤ 800 LOC; the change lands via Gerrit with `Verified +1` and `LLM-Review +1`.

file_impact.reason: publish token counts to CloudWatch + journald marker after a successful vote
offline tests for token aggregation + CloudWatch emission

compiled_state.title: Review bot: read _usage and emit token counts to CloudWatch
